commit.message:
fix: add database seed step to frontend-tests CI job (#16)

* fix: add database seed step to frontend-tests CI job

The frontend E2E tests fail in CI because the database is not populated.
The *.db files are gitignored, so the SQLite database doesn't exist in CI.
This adds a step to run seed_database.py after environment setup and before
Playwright tests, ensuring the API returns data during E2E test runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use working-directory for seed step to resolve module imports

Set working-directory to ./server so Python can resolve 'from models import ...'
the same way the test scripts do (by having server/ as CWD).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: seed database on app startup and simplify CI workflow

Instead of a separate seed step in CI, call seed_database() in app.py on
startup. The seed function is idempotent so it's safe to call every time.
This means the database is always populated when the Flask server starts,
whether in CI, local dev, or any other environment.

Also simplify the frontend-tests job to use the existing run-e2e-tests.sh
script, which already handles setup via Playwright's webServer config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore npm ci step before Playwright install in CI

Playwright browsers must be installed after npm ci, otherwise
@playwright/test package is not found.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Restore HTTP-based health server, make it opt-in (#6)

* fix: restore HTTP-based health server, make it opt-in

Revert the direct-upstream health probe introduced in #5, which opened
a second TCP connection to the Modbus device on every check. Many
Modbus devices only accept one concurrent connection, so the probe
interfered with normal proxy operation in production.

Restore the original passive HTTP health server that checks internal
connection state without touching upstream. Make it opt-in via
HEALTH_LISTEN so bare-binary users running multiple instances (the
root cause of #4) no longer hit port conflicts. The Dockerfile sets
HEALTH_LISTEN=:8080 so Docker users get health checks automatically.

Closes #4

* fix: normalize wildcard hosts in CheckHealth, isolate config test env

Co-authored-by: tma <4719+tma@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial plan (#178)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge pull request #130 from dchu3/fix/x402-v1-network-name

fix: decode X-PAYMENT header before sending to facilitator /settle

The settlePayment function was sending the raw base64 X-PAYMENT header
string to the facilitator, but the /settle endpoint expects a decoded
JSON object (matching the x402 SDK's HTTPFacilitatorClient.settle()
format). Also adds the required top-level x402Version field.

Before: { paymentPayload: 'eyJ4NDAy...' (base64 string) }
After:  { x402Version: 1, paymentPayload: { x402Version: 1, scheme: 'exact', ... } }

Added server-side error logging for settlement failures to aid debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: onecrawl-bindings-shared crate for cross-binding DRY

New crate centralizes complex business logic shared between NAPI and PyO3:
- agent::execute_chain — JS command chain executor (25 LOC → 1 call site)
- agent::stream_capture — screenshot + metadata capture
- auth::form_login — multi-step form authentication
- memory::{parse_category,store,recall,search} — agent memory with 8-branch category mapping
- network::{api_capture_start,api_capture_summary} — fetch interception JS templates

Both bindings now delegate to shared functions, eliminating duplicated
orchestration logic while keeping FFI wrappers thin.

Net: -115 LOC across bindings, +196 LOC shared (6 complex functions centralized)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: MSRV pin, version consistency, Minikube profile portability (#182)

Four review comments from the Rust 2024 edition / docs PR were left
unaddressed. This PR applies them.

## Changes

- **`Cargo.toml`** — Pin `rust-version = "1.85"` in
`[workspace.package]`; edition 2024 requires it and without it older
toolchains fail with opaque errors instead of a clear MSRV diagnostic.

- **`deploy/helm/trackone/README.md`**
- Revert example tag from `v0.1.0-alpha.8` → `v0.1.0-alpha.7` to match
actual `Chart.yaml` `appVersion` and workspace crate versions.
- Replace hardcoded `-p minikube` with `-p
${MINIKUBE_PROFILE:-minikube}`.

- **`deploy/k8s/local/README.md`** — Same
`${MINIKUBE_PROFILE:-minikube}` substitution in both `eval` blocks
(image build and sanity-check).

```bash
# Before
eval "$(minikube -p minikube docker-env)"

# After
eval "$(minikube -p ${MINIKUBE_PROFILE:-minikube} docker-env)"
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💬 We'd love your input! Share your thoughts on Copilot coding agent in
our [2 minute survey](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-survey).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: bilalobe <51460731+bilalobe@users.noreply.github.com>

[table_list_view_table] stabilize jest test (#254991)

## Summary

Closes https://github.com/elastic/kibana/issues/253562

Stabilizes the `created_by` filter Jest tests for `TableListViewTable`
by making user interactions more deterministic and splitting assertions
into smaller, focused test cases.

### Checklist

Check the PR satisfies following conditions. 

Reviewers should verify this PR satisfies this list as well.

- ~[ ] Any text added follows [EUI's writing
guidelines](https://elastic.github.io/eui/#/guidelines/writing), uses
sentence case text and includes [i18n
support](https://github.com/elastic/kibana/blob/main/src/platform/packages/shared/kbn-i18n/README.md)~
- ~[ ]
[Documentation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/kibana/master/development-documentation.html)
was added for features that require explanation or tutorials~
- ~[ ] [Unit or functional
tests](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/kibana/master/development-tests.html)
were updated or added to match the most common scenarios~
- ~[ ] If a plugin configuration key changed, check if it needs to be
allowlisted in the cloud and added to the [docker
list](https://github.com/elastic/kibana/blob/main/src/dev/build/tasks/os_packages/docker_generator/resources/base/bin/kibana-docker)~
- ~[ ] This was checked for breaking HTTP API changes, and any breaking
changes have been approved by the breaking-change committee. The
`release_note:breaking` label should be applied in these situations.~
- ~[ ] [Flaky Test
Runner](https://ci-stats.kibana.dev/trigger_flaky_test_runner/1) was
used on any tests changed~
- [x] The PR description includes the appropriate Release Notes section,
and the correct `release_note:*` label is applied per the
[guidelines](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/kibana/master/contributing.html#kibana-release-notes-process)
- [x] Review the [backport
guidelines](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1VyN5k91e5OVumlc0Gb9RPa3h1ewuPE705nRtioPiTvY/edit?usp=sharing)
and apply applicable `backport:*` labels.

commit.message:
feat(tui): add interactive bubbletea TUI for inGitDB repositories

When `ingitdb` is run without a subcommand inside an inGitDB repository
the CLI now launches a full-screen interactive terminal UI built with
github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea.

Home screen (2-panel layout):
- Left panel: scrollable list of root collections with muted directory
  paths and a '+ Add collection' button; ENTER navigates to the
  collection detail screen
- Right panel: welcome message, feature overview, and link to the
  GitHub repository

Collection screen (2-column layout):
- Narrow left column: collection schema (id, record file format,
  columns with types, sub-collections)
- Main right panel: records rendered as a table with ↑/↓ navigation;
  columns follow the schema's columns_order; ESC returns to home

Key bindings: ↑/↓ or k/j to navigate, ENTER to open, ESC to go back,
q / Ctrl+C to quit.

The TUI is only launched when stdout is a real TTY; piped output and
test environments fall through to the existing cobra help behaviour.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: disable nx cache for test step to ensure coverage files are written

Nx cache restores terminal output but not generated files (e.g. coverage/).
Adding --no-cache ensures lcov.info is always written to disk so Codecov
never reports 'unknown' coverage.

commit.message:
Fix CI: remove FSharp.Core duplicate causing NETSDK1005 on net9.0/net10.0

- Remove DisableImplicitFSharpCoreReference and explicit FSharp.Core 8.0.0
  PackageReference; let SDK supply appropriate FSharp.Core per TFM (net8/9/10).
  The duplicate caused NU1504 on macOS CI (sdk 10.x), which prevented NuGet
  from writing net9.0/net10.0 targets into project.assets.json, producing
  NETSDK1005 for those TFMs.
- Fix FS3873 deprecation in Widgets.fs: wrap range literals in seq { } for
  Set.ofSeq calls (affects net8.0 and net9.0 TFMs under newer F# compiler).
- Update README test count 2,630 -> 2,653 to pass CI badge check.
- Update PackageReleaseNotes for Sprint 68 features.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cycle 22 Phase 6: Full Identity Lifecycle + Vendor Admin Tools (#329)

* Initial plan

* feat: Cycle 22 Phase 6 - Full Identity Lifecycle + Admin Tools

Implement complete vendor identity lifecycle management:

Messages.Contracts:
- VendorUserInvitationResent - for resending pending invitations
- VendorUserInvitationRevoked - for revoking pending invitations

TenantManagement (commands, validators, handlers):
- SuspendVendorTenant - Active → Suspended with reason
- ReinstateVendorTenant - Suspended → Active (clears suspension data)
- TerminateVendorTenant - Any non-terminated → Terminated (terminal state)

UserInvitations (commands, validators, handlers):
- ResendVendorUserInvitation - regenerates token, extends expiry, increments count
- RevokeVendorUserInvitation - marks pending invitation as Revoked

UserManagement (new directory, commands, validators, handlers):
- DeactivateVendorUser - Active → Deactivated with last-admin protection
- ReactivateVendorUser - Deactivated → Active
- ChangeVendorUserRole - role change with last-admin demotion protection

VendorPortal compensation:
- VendorTenantTerminatedHandler - auto-rejects in-flight change requests

Integration tests:
- 16 new tests covering all happy paths and error cases
- All 42 tests pass (26 existing + 16 new)

* Address UX feedback: add Reason to TerminateVendorTenant, fix error message consistency, add EF migration

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add QA-required tests: not-found, validation, reinstate-terminated guard (5 new tests)

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add 10 edge case integration tests for VendorIdentity lifecycle (Cycle 22 Phase 6)

- Test 22-24: User operations (deactivate, change role, reactivate) on suspended tenants succeed (identity-level ops not blocked by tenant suspension)
- Test 25-27: User operations (deactivate, change role, invite) on terminated tenants succeed (validators check tenant existence, not status)
- Test 28: Consecutive resend invitations produce cryptographically distinct tokens
- Test 29: Change role to same role is a no-op that succeeds
- Test 30: Deactivating an already-deactivated user returns 400
- Test 31: Suspending a tenant with empty reason returns 400

Total: 31 tests (21 existing + 10 new), all passing

* Update documentation: CURRENT-CYCLE.md, CONTEXTS.md, retrospective, event-modeling for Phase 6 completion

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update exams/GH-300/GH-300-cheat-sheet.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: production-grade Node20 GitHub Action + release decoupling + correct download URLs (#7)

* Initial plan

* fix: broken GitHub Pages links, missing favicon, install.sh rename, enable manual releases

- scripts/install.sh: replace all nexusOrchestrator refs with nexus-orchestrator
  (GITHUB_REPO var, ARCHIVE name, URL comment, two log messages)
- docs/public/favicon.svg: create missing favicon (index.html referenced it → 404)
- docs/src/views/HomeView.vue: fix unstyled hero 'View on GitHub' <a> tag
  (add Tailwind classes, target=_blank, rel=noopener)
- .github/workflows/release.yml: add workflow_dispatch version input,
  propagate it through build & desktop build steps, allow publish job on
  manual dispatch (was blocked by startsWith(refs/tags/v) guard),
  pass tag_name/name to softprops/action-gh-release for atomic tag creation

Co-authored-by: el-j <2795534+el-j@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: GitHub Actions composite action, release decoupling, docs GitHub Actions section

action.yml (new):
- Composite action: download daemon → start → submit task → poll → stop
- Inputs: instruction/task_file, system_prompt (agency-agents chain),
  target_file, command, model, provider, openai/anthropic/copilot keys,
  timeout_seconds, start_daemon, daemon_url, nexus_version
- Outputs: task_id, status, logs
- Pure Python3 (no extra deps); cleanup step runs on always()

.github/workflows/release.yml:
- release job: needs [build] only (was [build, build-desktop])
- New release-desktop job: needs [build-desktop, release], if: always()
  && release succeeded && desktop builds succeeded
- Safer mv via find -maxdepth 1 -exec mv {} . instead of glob
- Explicit file list in release job (no wildcards)

docs/src/views/DownloadsView.vue:
- Desktop App cards: nexus-orchestrator-desktop-{os}-{arch}.* URLs
  (was pointing at CLI archives incorrectly)

docs/src/views/McpIntegrationView.vue:
- New #github-actions section: basic usage + agency-agents chaining example
- Commit step guarded by if: steps.nexus.outputs.status == 'COMPLETED'
- Key inputs reference table (12 rows)
- TOC entry added

commit.message:
JIT: Stop treating GT_LCLHEAP as inherently exceptional and remove dead StackOverflowException flag (#125362)

## Description

`GT_LCLHEAP` was previously marked as inherently exception-producing via
`GTF_EXCEPT` in the importer, and `OperExceptions` also reported
`StackOverflowException` for it. Based on review feedback, this change
removes that special exception treatment instead of trying to delete
`GT_LCLHEAP` nodes in liveness.

As a follow-up cleanup, the now-unused
`ExceptionSetFlags::StackOverflowException` enum value is also removed
from the JIT exception flag set.

### Changes

- **`importer.cpp`** — Stopped marking imported `GT_LCLHEAP` nodes with
`GTF_EXCEPT`. The node still gets `GTF_DONT_CSE`.

- **`gentree.cpp`** — Removed the `GT_LCLHEAP` case from
`GenTree::OperExceptions`, so it no longer reports
`StackOverflowException` as an inherent exception side effect.

- **`liveness.cpp`** — Reverted the earlier liveness-specific
dead-code-elimination handling for `GT_LCLHEAP`.

- **`morph.cpp`** — Updated the call-argument reordering logic to check
for `GT_LCLHEAP` explicitly in the places where its stack-depth behavior
still matters, rather than relying on `GTF_EXCEPT`.

- **`utils.h`** — Removed the dead
`ExceptionSetFlags::StackOverflowException` enum member after the
`GT_LCLHEAP` exception handling change made it unused.

- **Behavioral intent** — `GT_LCLHEAP` is no longer treated as
inherently exceptional solely because it may grow the stack. Any
exception-related behavior now comes from propagated operand flags
rather than special casing on the node itself, and the unused stack
overflow exception flag has been deleted from the JIT exception set.

## Testing

- `./build.sh clr.jit -rc release`
- `./build.sh libs -rc release -lc release`
- `src/tests/build.sh -Test JIT/Directed/localloc/localloc3_cs_r.csproj
x64 Release -priority1`

Focused validation succeeded for the affected JIT and localloc test
build path.

commit.message:
[CK TILE ENGINE] Add grouped_gemm operator to Tile Engine (gfx942/gfx950) (#4996)

## Motivation

The grouped_gemm CK Tile kernel exists (e.g.,
`example/17_grouped_gemm/`) but has no Tile Engine wrapper. Grouped GEMM
handles multiple independent GEMM problems with varying M/N/K dimensions
in a single kernel launch. This PR adds the Tile Engine infrastructure
for automated kernel generation, benchmarking, and profiling of grouped
GEMM kernels.

Jira: AICK-809

## Technical Details

- Created Tile Engine wrapper under `tile_engine/ops/gemm/grouped_gemm/`
following the `gemm_universal` template
- Files added: `CMakeLists.txt`, `grouped_gemm_common.hpp`,
`grouped_gemm_benchmark.hpp`, `grouped_gemm_profiler.hpp`,
`grouped_gemm_benchmark.py`, `grouped_gemm_benchmark_single.cpp`,
`grouped_gemm_instance_builder.py`, `configs/`
- Supported datatypes: fp16, fp8, bf16, bf8
- Supported layouts: rcr, rrr, ccr, crr
- Target GPUs: gfx942, gfx950
- CK Tile kernel: `ck_tile::GroupedGemmKernel` from
`include/ck_tile/ops/gemm/kernel/grouped_gemm_kernel.hpp`
- Instance builder extends `GemmKernelBuilder` base class
- Registered in `tile_engine/ops/gemm/CMakeLists.txt`
- Updated Jenkinsfile to build and benchmark grouped_gemm targets in CI
- Benchmark infrastructure includes JSON output, CSV export, and
verification support

## Test Plan

- CMake configure succeeds for grouped_gemm targets
- Kernel instance builder generates valid kernel headers for all
(datatype, layout) combinations
- At least one kernel binary compiles and runs per datatype/layout
combination
- Correctness passes with `--verify 1` on gfx942/gfx950

## Test Result



## Submission Checklist

- [ ] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

---------

commit.message:
feat: Agent debug panel showing prompt, LLM calls, skills applied

Debug info captured from agentic executor:
- System prompt (full text, collapsible)
- LLM call trace: iteration #, response type, tools called, duration
- Total LLM calls and tool calls count
- Skills applied list
- Model used

UI: '🔍 Agent Debug Info' collapsible panel after agentic execution
Shows stats grid (4 metrics) + skills badges + call trace table +
expandable system prompt. Helps customers understand agent decisions.
